name: liquibase
description: Liquibase is an open-source database change management and schema migration tool supporting XML, YAML, JSON, and SQL changelog formats across multiple database platforms

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Deploy all unrun changesets from the changelog file to the database. This is the primary command for applying schema changes.

options.name: --changelog-file
options.description: Path to the changelog file (XML, YAML, JSON, or SQL)

options.name: --contexts
options.description: Comma-separated list of contexts to execute

subcommands.name: updateSQL
subcommands.description: Generate the SQL that would be executed by the update command without actually applying it. Useful for review and audit purposes.

options.name: --output-file
options.description: Write SQL output to a file instead of stdout

subcommands.name: rollback
subcommands.description: Roll back changes to a specified tag, undoing all changesets applied after the tag was set.

subcommands.name: rollbackSQL
subcommands.description: Output the SQL that would be executed by a rollback to a given tag, without actually performing the rollback.

options.name: --output-file
options.description: Write SQL output to a file

subcommands.name: rollbackCount
subcommands.description: Roll back a specified number of the most recently applied changesets.

args.name: count
args.description: Number of changesets to roll back

subcommands.name: rollbackToDate
subcommands.description: Roll back all changesets that were applied after a specified date and time.

subcommands.name: generateChangeLog
subcommands.description: Generate a changelog file from the current database schema. Reverse-engineers the database into Liquibase changeset format.

options.name: --changelog-file
options.description: Output path for the generated changelog

options.name: --data-output-directory
options.description: Directory for CSV data export files

options.name: --diff-types
options.description: Types of objects to include (tables, views, columns, indexes, etc.)

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Compare two databases and output the differences. Useful for detecting schema drift between environments.

options.name: --output-schemas
options.description: Schemas to include in diff output

subcommands.name: diffChangeLog
subcommands.description: Compare two databases and generate a changelog file containing the changes needed to synchronize them.

options.name: --changelog-file
options.description: Output path for the diff changelog

subcommands.name: snapshot
subcommands.description: Capture a snapshot of the current database schema, outputting the full structure as JSON or YAML.

subcommands.name: snapshotReference
subcommands.description: Capture a snapshot of the reference database schema for comparison purposes.

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Display a list of changesets that have not been applied to the database yet (pending changes).

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed information about each pending changeset

subcommands.name: validate
subcommands.description: Validate the changelog for errors such as duplicate IDs, invalid preconditions, or missing files without connecting to the database.

options.name: --changelog-file
options.description: Path to the changelog file to validate

subcommands.name: changelogSync
subcommands.description: Mark all unrun changesets as already executed in the database. Useful when you want to skip applying changes that have already been applied manually.

subcommands.name: clearCheckSums
subcommands.description: Remove all stored checksums from the DATABASECHANGELOG table, forcing Liquibase to recalculate them on the next update.

subcommands.name: tag
subcommands.description: Tag the current database state with a label for future rollback reference points.

subcommands.name: tagExists
subcommands.description: Check whether a specific tag exists in the database changelog.

subcommands.name: history
subcommands.description: Display the deployment history of all changes applied to the database, including timestamps and changeset details.

subcommands.name: dropAll
subcommands.description: Drop all database objects in the configured schemas. WARNING: This is destructive and removes all tables, views, and procedures.

subcommands.name: futureRollbackSQL
subcommands.description: Generate the SQL that would be needed to rollback all pending (unrun) changesets, providing a preview of rollback scripts.

subcommands.name: unexpectedChangeSets
subcommands.description: List changesets that exist in the database but are not present in the current changelog file. Indicates potential drift.

subcommands.name: dbDoc
subcommands.description: Generate HTML documentation from the changelog and the current database schema, creating a browsable database documentation site.

args.name: outputDirectory
args.description: Output directory for the generated documentation

globalOptions.name: --changelog-file
globalOptions.description: Path to the root changelog file

globalOptions.name: --driver
globalOptions.description: Fully qualified JDBC driver class name

globalOptions.name: --liquibase-schema-name
globalOptions.description: Schema to use for Liquibase tables

globalOptions.name: --database-changelog-table-name
globalOptions.description: Name of the changelog tracking table

globalOptions.name: --database-changelog-lock-table-name
globalOptions.description: Name of the changelog lock table

globalOptions.name: --log-level
globalOptions.description: Log level: OFF, SEVERE, WARNING, INFO, FINE, DEBUG

globalOptions.name: --log-file
globalOptions.description: File to write log output to
